pattern: BFS/DFS on a tree tracking probability; once at a leaf or forced to stay, the probability freezes.
hints: Build an adjacency list and BFS from node 1, carrying the current probability through each step.
When you reach the target node, note how many unvisited neighbours it has: if zero (leaf) or t is exhausted, return the accumulated probability; otherwise the frog will keep jumping away.
Use DFS with remaining-time and parent tracking; the probability multiplies by 1/(number of unvisited children) at each hop.

edge_inputs.description: single node, target is node 1, t=1
frog reaches target exactly at t but target has no children (stays)
frog reaches target at t=1 out of 3 neighbors
target unreachable in t steps, expect 0
n=2 line, target=2, t exceeds depth (frog stays at leaf)
target has children remaining so frog cannot stay at t reached
deep path, frog cannot reach target even with enough t

stress.description: random balanced tree of 50 nodes, t=10
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    edges = []
    for i in range(2, n + 1):
        parent = rng.randint(1, i - 1)
        edges.append([parent, i])
    t = 10
    target = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [n, edges, t, target]
